title: Projektmanager TGA / MEP (KG 400 – Elektro & HLSK) – Bauherrenseite (m/w/d)
location: Remote (Germany) with regular site visits across Europe
required_skills: Degree in HKLS / Gebäudetechnik (building services engineering) or comparable vocational training
Experience in TGA technical site management (Fachbauleitung), specialist planning, or comparable role
Competence in plan review (Planprüfung), quality assurance, and project coordination
Good technical understanding across all project phases from planning to handover
bonus_skills: Experience with FF&E project management (Sound, IT, Gastro, KeyCard procurement)
missing_skills: Zero TGA/MEP engineering background or education
No Elektro or HLSK technical knowledge
No plan review (Planprüfung) or quality assurance in construction context
No experience with KG 400 cost groups or technical building specifications
No hotel construction or hospitality project background
German B1 likely insufficient for technical German-language construction coordination
core_responsibilities: Accompany hotel projects across Europe in TGA scope (KG 400)
Technical responsibility for Elektro and HLSK trade packages
Review external specialist plans from Ruby perspective and integrate requirements early
Ensure technical and quality standards are consistently implemented
Challenge and advise specialist planners, investors, and project partners at eye level
Accompany projects from planning through implementation to commissioning and handover

recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — this is a building services engineering role requiring technical qualifications in HKLS/TGA that the candidate entirely lacks
This is one of the clearest non-matches in the batch: the role is for a trained building services engineer
No amount of project management experience bridges the technical engineering gap here
summary: Match score is 3.3 (poor_match). This is a building services engineering (TGA/MEP) role requiring specialist technical qualifications in electrical and HVAC systems, hands-on construction site experience, and the ability to technically review plans. The candidate has no engineering background whatsoever. This is fundamentally a technical specialist role, not a project management role, and represents one of the worst fits in this batch. Do not apply under any circumstances.
